review_title: Sigma plus Snowflake is a revelation!
description: It has exceeded my expectations in ease of use and the productivity my team has been able to achieve.
author: Andy R.
pros: I love the secure cloud hosting, the ease of connecting to data sources, the fact that it queries the data directly, but most I like the grid based view of data that is always at hand. Being able to view, sort, filter, and create calculations on the data in a familiar spreadsheet-like interface is genius. The “Column Details” feature allows me to quickly understand the range of values in the system.
cons: I’ve generated several feature requests for areas we’d like to see more detail.

description: I love Sigma and would recommend this software to anyone
author: Isabella C.
pros: Sigma Computing is a really powerful data analytics tool. The customer support is superb, since you can resolve roadblocks in real time. It really makes a difference especially when first learning the software. I am very very pleased with the visualization and data manipulation abilities of Sigma Computing.
cons: Sigma requires a learning curve of at least 2 weeks for new users. If users don't commit the time to learning, they will continually put off mastering and using the software, even if familiar tools like Excel and Google Sheets are less capable and take more time to create visualizations in.

description: Overall I have access to more than our previous reporting system, which is a positive. However, as self serve as it is I wish connecting tables and validating was more straightforward as a user.
author: martha Q.
pros: I like how customizable it is. From a good basic report I can move things around to suit my needs. In addition it has many options for how to export, which is great as I use excel for more complex files & google sheets for collaborating & sharing findings.
cons: Load times to get to a report or filter can be killer for some reports that pull from many data sources, and have large amounts of information. The permissions are a bit annoying when a report is created for me by an admin, but I am the report owner (as it was made for my department), but I can't make my own changes (w/o admin privlidges) and archive the main one. In these cases, I have to request the admin to make the changes, or make a copy and publish my own report and again contact the admin to delete or move the report so that mine is the one the team goes to first.

description: I am obsessed with Sigma, by far my favorite BI tool I've used. It let me do calculations myself that would usually require whole cycles of data requests, and affords me much more flexibility.
author: Nicholas M.
pros: * Ability to pull any data I need or want * Complexity of calculations possible while being easy to use * Seriously those grouping levels are VERY powerful
cons: * Pivot tables are the one thing they haven't aced yet - you can make them but then to calculate cased on them you need to make a new dataset and then a new sheet. You can do pretty much all the same work with just levels though so I don't miss them too often. * I wish my default permissions could be "public" - I'm never doing anything private, so why is that the default? * Maps could use some work. I want to color-map countries, not just display dots

review_title: The BI tool of the future
description: Love it. We previously had Looker as our BI tool and this is a HUGE improvement over that tool. Ease of use, clarity of features, flexibility of the system are all significantly improved. Couldn't be happier with our switch, I know my whole team would say the same thing.
author: Jordan S.
pros: Ease of use, especially the ability to write custom SQL queries to build datasets and reports. The visualization builder is also very user friendly and has made throwing together dashboards for internal or external use significantly easier. Saves me and my colleagues a ton of time!
cons: There have been some features identified by the team that we need for Enterprise-level software that were missing, many have been addressed though. A major bugbear of mine is that the filters don't seem to react to each other if there are multiple on a dashboard, they filter independently of other inputs.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Using Sigma as a Software developer
description: I think is a nice tool, is easy to use and it's fast. You can load your data and start analyzing it very fast, also, it's a nice tool for non technical users, but when I need more features or power I rather to use Data Studio
author: Tania R.
pros: Is an easy to use software, I liked that it's very fast, I don't have to wait to start seeing the data
cons: It needs more features, specially to migrate the worksheets to different environments, also it has a very limited number of functions. Sometimes the error messages are not very descriptives, so we can't know what's happening or how to solve it (we had a worksheet with an error like: "the joins are not efficient", something like that, so that message was kind of useless)

description: As a data analyst, I've have had a lot of fun building dashboards and reports with Sigma. It's very flexible and has great usability
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - You can easily add data sources, and then create datasets with the columns you need - It also lets you format the columns and process the data by using formulas, very similar to what you would do in a spreadsheet, but more streamlined - You can easily join different datasets and filter data - The new dashboards are very easy to use, with a clear interface to add widgets, filters and views - They also have quick and effective customer support
cons: - It can be a little intimidating if you are not familiar with spreadsheets
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Quick route to collaborative BI (especially for Excel users)
description: Sigma got my business to the point of insightful, collaborative BI in a couple of weeks, and it instantly was our most critical performance monitoring tool.
author: John M.
pros: Love the dataset -> spreadsheet -> dashboard usage model Seamless integration with Snowflake
cons: User admin/roles functionality is not as easy and consistent to administer as it should be

description: So far, I have loved it. For a month of experience, I feel very empowered to create the reports necessary to drive the business.
author: Gary B.
pros: Love the simplicity of creating reoccurring reports and setting them up for automated delivery.
cons: Some of the more complex formulas can be difficult to create, however the support has helped immensely. Only other con would be some of the output formats. one of the largest issues I still have is to output to a format that includes the entire list of (pick a column)
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: This has been a net positive for our org.
author: Michell B.
pros: Sigma allows viewers for free, even viewers from outside your organization.
cons: The dashboard feature is not fully built out - many features seem to be missing. For example, I can't put percents on a pie chart.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Kyle T.
pros: Compared to competitors I found Sigma to be much more straightforward with regard to database connections and dashboard building. No complex data warehousing layer, or reporting databases to instrument / sync, it simply reads from whatever db you assign and syncs in real time. While there is still a learning curve, the excel-like functions make it friendlier to business users who are not familiar with SQL to enable them to build custom reports rather than only viewing. While there isn't a "team" assigned to you for onboarding like other tools offer, I have found adhoc email and chat support to be good enough, and certainly worth the $ saved compared to other 'implementation costs' that often come with or are built in to the pricing of competitive products.
cons: The team/user permissioning and management could be more straightforward.

review_title: Decent features, need to cater to code-less users
author: Jad H.
pros: the customer service chat button was nice
cons: the way of drag and drop report building is not intuitive

author: Austin H.
pros: Ease of use and visibility is great! Love the data that we are able to pull and how easy the interface is to use.
cons: There are a few features that I would love to see added. Mainly that when you are working with large data sets, it can be hard on the system and lead to slow processing time.

description: I have been able to expand my personal skillset with sigma and work towards better data insights.
author: Alec P.
pros: Sigma offers a great way for me to explore tables in our database and visualize those insights. Building worksheets and converting those sheets into dashboards have been very valuable for me.
cons: There is a learning curve here. It may take a while to get completely comfortable using the product. Once you do, the possibilities for data analytics expand.

review_title: Great analytics product, easy to set up and use
author: Kevin K.
pros: Awesome team behind the product, always responsive and helpful. They've taught me a lot not only about their tool but also about data analytics.
cons: Some UI elements could be improved, but once you know where to find certain things you'll pick it up.

review_title: Good BI for basic data visualization
author: Yuting K.
pros: 1. excel like so it's easy to pick up 2. can connect to the live database and without writing SQL query 3. can share the worksheet and schedule to send out the worksheet via email
cons: 1. it's not easy to use if you want to do a complicated report: once you aggregate multiple worksheets / refer other worksheet and apply multiple filters, it's hard to verify your result and make it in the correct format. 2. the articles to discuss the Sigma features or best practice are not comprehensive.

author: Virgil S.
pros: I really like how easily Sigma connects with our snowflake databases, and facilitates analysis. We are growing our data and analytics practice, and Sigma helps me to respond to the innumerable ad hoc questions from the business, while allowing our team to advance our capabilities.
cons: Currently, I write SQL to execute the LIKE function when filtering on field values. It would be great to have this feature available with wild cards in the filter part of the software.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Increase the visibility of your data across teams and find your own answer to data questions
description: I am slowly getting up to speed with Sigma but can see already that as I commit to it we will greatly increase the speed at which my team can answer questions we have regarding all of the data we are collecting. There will be a day where I will no longer have a need for Excel and Google Sheets.
author: Frank P.
pros: Really like the ease of being able to expose our data across teams and allow each individual user to be able to answer the questions they have with the tools supplied, grouping and filters being two of the easiest and most useful for me.
cons: Up to the this point the visualizations aka charts have been difficult for me to create, but I will admit I haven't committed to taking a deep dive into learning. Also would like to be able to annotate from within a report (eg. cell) rather than at the top level within the description.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Heather C.
pros: As a marketer, Sigma is an incredibly valuable tool that allows my team to aggregate data to inform our decisions and scale up. I like the ability to drill app activity down to the user level in order to identify trends and specific user behaviors.
cons: It can be very overwhelming! Not instantly intuitive but not hard to learn either. My biggest complaint is that it was often very slow.

review_title: A vast improvement over our previous product
description: Using Sigma as a tool to prioritize asset service and inspection has been a game changer. Much better visualization tools than the competitor's product we previously used.
author: Blake T.
pros: It is much faster and easier to read than our old reporting software
cons: I would like the ability to modify the sort on the fly. Currently an admin has to adjust it, if it is needed
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: We're starting to think of problems we can solve, but having disorganization in your company's data doesn't make it easier.
author: Betsy W.
pros: The ease of combining data sets and manipulating data in a way that makes sense to me.
cons: It's not super user-friendly. I don't have a ton of experience with SQL and I feel like I am limited.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It is a good software but would be so much better if loading time could be reduced
author: adya S.
pros: Different custom filters that can be built in, personalization
cons: Loading time- makes the process very hard- refreshing the grid takes forever
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: So far it gets the job done.
author: KEONA S.
pros: I like the training resources that are provided within the program. It's also very user friendly
cons: I'm still learning the system.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Chen G.
pros: constant update and improve; great online support and customer service.
cons: the navigation bar needs improvement, too many clicks every time.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Emma M.
pros: Ability to connect to multiply databases
cons: Not enough flexibility to edit tables like excel
